properties.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 22 at 7:21PM MDT until July 22 at 7:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
The storm which prompted the warning has weakened below severe
limits, and no longer poses an immediate threat to life or property.
Therefore, the warning will be allowed to expire. However, gusty
winds are still possible with this thunderstorm.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 7:18PM MDT until July 22 at 10:15PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...East Central Lincoln County in east central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 1015 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 718 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area. Between 0.5 and 1 inch of rain has
fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Coon Creek, Hell Creek, South Fork Republican River, Big Sandy
Creek and Sevenmile Creek.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Hugo and Genoa.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 7:08PM MDT until July 22 at 10:15PM MDT by NWS Goodland KS
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...Southwestern Kit Carson County in east central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 1015 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 708 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 3 inches of rain have
fallen.
- Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are expected over
the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Flagler.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 7:02PM MDT until July 22 at 9:15PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...East Central Lincoln County in east central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 915 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 701 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms, with additional thunderstorms currently entering
the area. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly
in the advisory area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
South Fork Republican River and Hell Creek.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Arriba.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 7:00PM MDT until July 22 at 8:45PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...Northeastern Lincoln County in east central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 845 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 658 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms, with additional
thunderstorms entering the area. Minor flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 2
inches of rain have fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Hell Creek and Dugout Creek.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Mainly rural areas of Northeastern Lincoln County.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 6:55PM MDT until July 22 at 8:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has extended the

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Lee and PL Gulch burn scar in...
Central Rio Blanco County in northwestern Colorado...

* Until 800 PM MDT.

* At 655 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain over the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scar. Between 0.5 and
1.5 inches of rain have fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow
moving through the Lee and PL Gulch burn scars. The debris flow can
consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding in and around the Lee and PL
Gulch burn scars.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around
the Lee and PL Gulch burn scars.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of Central Rio Blanco County
Lee and PL Gulch burn scars

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 6:42PM MDT until July 22 at 9:45PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
FFWPUB

The National Weather Service in Pueblo has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Southern Aspen Acres Burn Scar in...
West Central Pueblo County in southeastern Colorado...

* Until 945 PM MDT.

* At 642 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain over the Southern Aspen Acres Burn Scar. The expected
rainfall rate is 0.4 to 0.8 inches in 1 hour. Flash flooding is
ongoing or expected to begin shortly. Excessive rainfall over the
burn scar will result in debris flow. The debris flow can consist
of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms producing
flash flooding in and around the Southern Aspen Acres Burn
Scar.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around the
Southern Aspen Acres Burn Scar.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Rye, San Isabel and Colorado City.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 5:26PM MDT until July 22 at 8:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Northwestern San Miguel County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 830 PM MDT.

* At 526 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.3 and 0.6 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 4 inches in 1
hour. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Slick Rock and Egnar and areas along Highway 141.

properties.headline: Flood Warning issued July 22 at 5:02PM MDT until July 22 at 8:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban area and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of west central Colorado, including the
following county, Mesa.

* WHEN...Until 800 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying
and flood-prone locations is imminent or occurring.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 502 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the warned area. Between 0.5 and 1 inch of rain
has fallen.
- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Indian Wash, Leach Creek and Colorado River.
Additional rainfall amounts of 0.3 to 0.8 inches are possible
in the warned area.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Grand Junction, Fruitvale, Clifton and Orchard Mesa.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:49PM MDT until July 22 at 8:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

West Central Dolores County in southwestern Colorado...
North Central Montezuma County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 800 PM MDT.

* At 449 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.4 and 0.8 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 1 to 3 inches in 1

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Dove Creek, Cahone, Yellow Jacket and Lewis including areas along
Highway 491.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:34PM MDT until July 22 at 7:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...

* At 434 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 11:04AM MDT until July 22 at 9:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Fire is
possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Fire in Ouray County Colorado, including
Dexter and Cutler Creeks and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 900 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1057 AM MDT, The probability of heavy rainfall will
increase after 12 PM this afternoon, lasting through the
evening. If thunderstorms develop over the Gold Mountain burn
scar, debris flows are likely within Cutler and Dexter Creek
drainages, including Highway 550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
